CROSS COMPLAINT FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT IRELAND, MICHAEL AN INDIVIDUAL IRELAND INTERIOR SYSTEMS, INC. AS TO CROSS DEFENDANT VAN BEMMEL, MICHELLE AN INDIVIDUAL VAN BEMMEL, MICHAEL AN INDIVIDUAL VAN BEMMEL, GLENDA AN INDIVIDUAL WOODS, RICHARD AN INDIVIDUAL CARLOS INTERIOR SYSTEMS, INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORAT ION VAN BEMMEL, SERENA ROES 1-50, INCLUSIVE

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANTS MICHELLE VAN BEMMEL, MICHAEL VAN BEMMEL, GLENDA VAN BEMMEL, RICHARD WOODS, CARLOS INTERIOR SYSTEMS, INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORAT AND SERENA VAN BEMMEL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO MICHAEL IRELAND (CCP 430.10(B)), AND OVERRULED AS TO ALL REMAINING GROUNDS AND CAUSES OF ACTION. THE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS; CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ALLEGE UNAUTHORIZED TRANSFERS OCCURRING UNTIL FEBRUARY 2013. (FIRST AM. CROSS-COMPLAINT, 22.) FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, MICHAEL IRELAND VOLUNTARILY WITHDRAWS HIS CAUSE OF ACTION AS AN INDIVIDUAL ONLY. (OPP., P. 3:22-23.) FOR THE SECOND - SIXTH, EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION, ALTHOUGH UNJUST ENRICHMENT IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANTS HAVE PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE RESTITUTION. (DURELL V. SHARP HEALTHCARE (2010) 183 CAL.APP.4TH 1350, 1370.) FOR THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANTS HAVE PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE CONVERSION BASED ON A SPECIFIC, IDENTIFIABLE SUM OF MONEY. (PCO, INC. V. CHRISTENSEN, MILLER, FINK, JACOBS, GLASER, WEIL & SHAPIRO, LLP (2007) 150 CAL.APP.4TH 384, 395.) FOR THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANTS HAVE PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE AN ACTUAL, PRESENT CONTROVERSY BASED ON THE TWO AGREEMENTS. ON THE ONE HAND, IN THE COMMON STOCK SUBSCRIPTION AGREEMENT (FIRST AM. CROSS-COMPLAINT, EXHIBIT A), MICHELLE VAN BEMMEL'S SHARES OF STOCK WERE TO BE HELD IN A VOTING TRUST UNTIL HER LOAN WAS PAID OFF. ON THE OTHER HAND, IN THE VOTING TRUST AGREEMENT (ID., AT EXHIBIT B), THE AGREEMENT WAS TO EXPIRE ON SEPTEMBER 1, 2012. THUS, AN ACTUAL, PRESENT CONTROVERSY EXISTS BETWEEN THE PARTIES AS TO THEIR RIGHTS AND DUTIES UNDER THE TWO AGREEMENTS. (SEE FIRST AM. COMPLAINT, EXHIBITS A, B.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj; Index
Off Calendar Jan-08-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ANNETTE WAGNER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Denied. Plaintiff met her initial burden as to all three causes of action. Defendant has raised triable issues of material fact as to all three causes of action. As to the breach of contract cause of action, the Declaration of Michael Ireland raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Michelle and Michael Van Bemmel had authority to contract on behalf of Ireland Interior Systems. (M. Ireland Decl., pars. 4, 6, 11.) The existence of agency, as well as the extent of authority of an agent, are questions of fact. (Wickham v. Southland Corp. (1985) 168 Cal.App.3d 49, 55.) As to the unjust enrichment/restitution cause of action, the Declaration of Michael Ireland raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Ireland Interior Systems knowingly accepted the benefit. (M. Ireland Decl., par. 11; see Hernandez v. Lopez (2009) 180 Cal.App.4th 932, 938-39.) The Declaration of Jeri Ireland also raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Ireland Interior Systems benefitted from the funds. (J. Ireland Decl., pars. 4-5, 6-8.) As to the common counts cause of action, the Declaration of Michael Ireland raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether there was a valid statement of indebtedness between Plaintiff and Ireland Interior Systems. (M. Ireland Decl., pars. 4, 6, 11.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj; Index

MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS MICHELLE VAN BEMMEL, MICHAEL VAN BEMMEL, GLENDA VAN BEMMEL, RICHARD WOODS, ANNETTE WAGNER, AND CARLOS INTERIOR SYSTEMS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is continued to January 8, 2015, to give the opposing party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of all opposing papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. The court has the moving papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

Off Calendar May-28-2014 Reset From May-28-14 To Jun-04-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS DEFENDANTS MICHELLE VAN BEMMEL, MICHAEL VAN BEMMEL, GLENDA VAN BEMMEL, RICHARD WOODS, CARLOS INTERIOR SYSTEMS, INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORAT AND SERENA VAN BEMMEL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST
AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained, without leave to amend, as to the first cause of action as to Michael Ireland (CCP 430.10(b)), and overruled as to all remaining grounds and causes of action. The claims are not barred by the statute of limitations; Cross-Complainants allege unauthorized transfers occurring until February 2013. (First Am. Cross-Complaint, 22.) For the first cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, Michael Ireland voluntarily withdraws his cause of action as an individual only. (Opp., p. 3:22-23.) For the second - sixth, eighth causes of action, although unjust enrichment is not a cause of action, Cross-Complainants have pled sufficient facts to allege restitution. (Durell v. Sharp Healthcare (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 1350, 1370.) For the seventh cause of action, Cross-Complainants have pled sufficient facts to allege conversion based on a specific, identifiable sum of money. (PCO, Inc. v. Christensen, Miller, Fink, Jacobs, Glaser, Weil & Shapiro, LLP (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 384, 395.) For the ninth cause of action, Cross-Complainants have pled sufficient facts to allege an actual, present controversy based on the two agreements. On the one hand, in the Common Stock Subscription Agreement (First Am. Cross-Complaint, Exhibit A), Michelle Van Bemmel's shares of stock were to be held in a voting trust until her loan was paid off. On the other hand, in the Voting Trust Agreement (id., at Exhibit B), the Agreement was to expire on September 1, 2012. Thus, an actual, present controversy exists between the parties as to their rights and duties under the two agreements. (See First Am. Complaint, Exhibits A, B.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
